notes: DDR5-6400 spent the last 7-14 days anchored by stable 64GB and 128GB values, while 32GB remained weak on exact public anchors and 16GB stayed absent.
DDR5-5600 remained structurally split at 32GB across low retail and high distributor bands; this is a persistent outlier_only problem, not a no_public_sample problem.
PCIe4 TLC has been the steadiest category over the last 7-14 days, with the main task being validation rather than repricing discovery.
PCIe5 TLC nominal coverage stayed decent, but the important underlying issue remains 30.72TB anchor decay: the old lower-band signal has not returned cleanly since 2026-04-13.
PCIe4 QLC stayed fully covered, but high-capacity low clusters remained noisy; stable premium public bands still exist for 61.44TB and 122.88TB.

noDirectSampleSources: 16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
outlierOnlySources: A-Tech/Newegg/CDW Kingston low cluster vs Provantage/CDW Micron high cluster for 32GB

noDirectSampleSources: Provantage KCMYXRUG30T7 exact public tile still missing

keyChangesVsYesterday: DDR5-5600 32GB gained another low-side public sample: CDW now shows Kingston KTL-TS556D8-32G at $895.99, reinforcing that the slot is still outlier_only rather than no_public_sample.
No tracked representative value changed versus 2026-04-15; the current market state is stability in main bands plus persistent structural splits in DDR5-5600 32GB and PCIe5 TLC 30.72TB.
Mouser remained inaccessible for high-capacity Solidigm P5336 checks, so this source continues to strengthen parser_gap classification rather than adding pricing evidence.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB remain stable, 96GB is still visible after the 2026-04-13 recovery, but 32GB still lacks a clean exact anchor and 16GB remains absent.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB remains structurally split across low retail and high distributor bands, and the new CDW Kingston low-side sample reinforces fragmentation rather than convergence.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: This remains the most stable and fully covered category, with no evidence of band breakdown or stale representative values.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: Lower capacities remain observable, but 30.72TB still lacks a trustworthy single representative band because the old lower anchor remains stale while reproducible Micron high-band quotes persist.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Main premium public bands remain stable, but high-capacity low clusters are still too noisy to justify a one-shot bottom-call.

gaps.note: Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
A-Tech, Newegg, and CDW Kingston form a low cluster near $896-$990, while Provantage/CDW Micron remain in a much higher distributor band.
The previously preferred lower-band anchor still did not repeat cleanly; current evidence remains split between a sold-out low cluster and reproducible Micron high-band quotes.

validationNotes: CDW Kingston 32GB DDR5-5600 is useful not because it solves the category, but because it further proves the 32GB slot is structurally split.
Mouser remains a parser/access problem for QLC supplementation, so repeated failures there should not be misread as no_public_sample.
No script output was used as a substitute for research judgement; representative values were kept unchanged because no new clean evidence justified a rewrite.
